title: phyllanthus fraguensis
intro: Phyllanthus fraguensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae, commonly known as the West Indian gooseberry or Achiote. It is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce inflammation, treat fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The plant is also rich in antioxidants, which can help protect against cell damage and promote overall health, making it beneficial for cardiovascular health and immune system function. In herbal preparations, Phyllanthus fraguensis is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in traditional medicine practices, to harness its therapeutic properties.
